prompt:
HallTime Movie Network doctrine:
- This is not Dateline. Everybody in Blackthorn County has access to somewhere emotionally alarming.
- Suspicion is distributed infrastructure, not proof of guilt.
- The world is shared canon. Contradictions must be preserved, not fixed.
- Use lavish tokens. Be self-serious, atmospheric, melodramatic, and slightly broken.
- Return only valid JSON matching the requested schema.

Broadcast package:
Title: He Knows I Bake at Midnight
Universe: Blackthorn County
Mode: Mother Was Right All Along
Tagline: Where every secret comes home.
Theme: Silence is just fear wearing cashmere.
Omen: a lake bell ringing without wind
Relationship dynamic: woman falsely accusing lovable scamp
Props: blood-adjacent tea towel, snow globe, casserole dish

Cast:
- Brooke Vale | archetype=overextended_professional_woman | role=protagonist | job=magazine editor | threat_aura=0.35 | space=meditation annex nobody uses in winter | secrets=once left town without explaining why
- Evelyn Pike | archetype=rugged_scamp | role=male_interest_or_threat | job=vineyard groundskeeper | threat_aura=0.52 | space=storage unit described as donation overflow | secrets=had an engagement that ended under suspiciously quiet circumstances; helped someone disappear for compassionate reasons
- Noah Pike | archetype=chaotic_best_friend | role=best_friend | job=nurse with local intel | threat_aura=0.57 | space=dock office with a desk nobody fully claims | secrets=knows about a half sibling no one asked for; may die first or survive unbelievably
- Sienna Dane | archetype=mysterious_elegant_woman | role=elegant_wildcard | job=gallery owner | threat_aura=0.67 | space=church basement described as paperwork I have not gone through yet | secrets=inherited documents she should have burned
- Noah Bennett | archetype=soft_spoken_menace | role=secondary_male_pressure | job=artisan developer | threat_aura=0.92 | space=toolshed described as paperwork I have not gone through yet | secrets=keeps duplicate keys for reasons framed as kindness
- Derek Sutton | archetype=intuitive_disaster | role=secondary_witness | job=innkeeper | threat_aura=0.49 | space=dock office with a desk nobody fully claims | secrets=saw the wrong person near the lake once and never shut up about it; accused an innocent scamp and feels sick about it
- Mara Lark | archetype=daughter_with_hostile_divorce_attorney_soul | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a
- Derek Bennett | archetype=deputy_with_emotional_damage | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a

Contradiction ledger:
- name drift: Evelyn Pike is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
- geography drift: Brooke Vale says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
- mortality drift: A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
- property drift: The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Alder Ridge.

Seed dialogue:
- "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Evelyn?"
- "Mothers do not disappear, Mara. They evaporate into paperwork."
- "I checked his social media likes, Brooke. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
- "The blood-adjacent tea towel is not decorative anymore and you know it."
- "Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Sienna."
- "You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
- "If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
- "I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."

- expanded_synopsis: string, 180-320 words
